commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is maintainer review plus contributor-provided real behavior proof for the Codex heartbeat runtime path.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the patch does not touch dependencies or workflows, and registered-only tools still fail closed at execution time. commands.review_summary:
The PR splits Codex dynamic tools into durable thread-registered specs and current-turn available specs so `heartbeat_respond` can stay in the thread schema without being advertised or executable on normal turns.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can be checked by running a normal Codex app-server turn, then a heartbeat turn, then a normal turn and observing the dynamic-tool fingerprint change because `heartbeat_respond` is only present on heartbeat turns. I did not execute the harness because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is maintainer review plus contributor-provided real behavior proof for the Codex heartbeat runtime path.

target.title: fix(codex): stabilize heartbeat dynamic tool schema
target.body:
## Summary

- Register a durable Codex dynamic tool schema set separately from the tools actually callable on the current turn.
- Keep `heartbeat_respond` in the thread schema so normal -> heartbeat -> normal turns reuse the same Codex thread, while normal turns still deny heartbeat tool execution explicitly.
- Keep prompt-facing tool manifests, system prompt reports, context-engine available-tool sets, and trajectory context scoped to tools available for the current turn.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "durable tool schema|registers heartbeat response durably"`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:changed` reached extension typecheck + extension test typecheck, then failed on pre-existing/unrelated OpenRouter lint in `extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts` (`unicorn/no-useless-fallback-in-spread`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex thread rotation caused by per-turn dynamic tool surface changes when heartbeat turns expose `heartbeat_respond` and normal turns do not.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Codex app-server unit harness, and local gateway launchd service.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the targeted Vitest regression above, Codex-only oxlint, full `pnpm build`, and restarted the local gateway with `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway`.

Evidence after fix: The new run-attempt regression starts a normal turn, resumes for a heartbeat turn, then resumes for another normal turn; it asserts one `thread/start` and two `thread/resume` calls while `heartbeat_respond` remains registered in the initial dynamic tool schema.

Observed result after fix: Targeted tests passed, Codex lint passed, build passed, and launchd reports `ai.openclaw.gateway` running.

What was not tested: A live production heartbeat against the deployed Telegram topic after the patch; broad `pnpm check:changed` is blocked by unrelated OpenRouter lint currently on the branch baseline.
target.author: jalehman
